text: Fill in the blanks about midbrain anatomy.
questions: The midbrain is divided into three regions: the *tectum* dorsally, the *tegmentum* in the middle, and the cerebral *peduncles/crus cerebri* ventrally.
In Weber syndrome, a lesion in the midbrain damages the *CN III/oculomotor* nerve fascicles (causing ipsilateral palsy) and the *corticospinal* tract (causing contralateral hemiplegia).
The substantia nigra has two parts: *pars compacta* which produces dopamine, and pars *reticulata* which provides GABAergic output to the thalamus.
The *superior/superior colliculus* is involved in visual reflexes while the *inferior/inferior colliculus* is an auditory relay centre.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
